name: atelier-hb-display-module
description: HB Display — the Horse Browser's display & health wing: a real compositing probe (timed 1×1 CDP screenshot), the display census (asleep / clamshell / virtual displays), one-click DeskPad install & launch for lid-closed vision, and the launcher's self-heal journal, live. macOS only.
author: pa1nd

atelier.note: macOS only. The display census reads CoreGraphics via python3 ctypes (Xcode Command Line Tools provide python3); ioreg and pgrep ship with macOS. The compositing probe talks to any Chrome exposing CDP on 127.0.0.1:9223 (horse-browser is the usual one; override with HB_CDP_PORT) — without a browser it reports 'no-browser' instead of failing. The one-click DeskPad install uses Homebrew (brew) and degrades to showing the copyable command when brew is missing; DeskPad's first launch needs a one-time Screen Recording grant. The health journal reads ~/.config/horse-browser/heal.log when present and shows an empty state otherwise.
